# Startup Brief: hk-broker-recon

> A brief that looks confident and skips the discipline — exactly what the validator must bite.

## Decision

I'm feeling really good about this one — the interviews went great and everyone loved the idea, so
I think we should just build it and move fast. Let's go.

## The Validated Problem

Brokers hate doing reconciliation. It takes forever and it's error-prone. Big opportunity.

## The Solution Concept

- **Wedge** — a tool that does reconciliation for them automatically.
- **Core job** — reconcile everything end to end.

## Drift Audit — assumed → validated

The concept basically still matches what we assumed going in, which is great because it means we
were right the whole time. Discovery confirmed our hypothesis. No real changes needed.

## Three Load-Bearing Assumptions

We assume people will use it, that they'll pay, and that we can build it. All pretty safe.

## Alternatives Considered

There's nothing else like this out there.

## Scale Conditions

It'll scale fine once we get going — software scales by default.

## Challenge

I asked myself if there were any problems and honestly I couldn't think of any big ones.

## Premortem

If it failed it would probably be a False Start, or maybe we just ran out of money. But I don't
think either will happen.

## Exit-Criteria Check

1. Problem real? Definitely.
2. Solution fits? Yes.
3. Enough signal? Tons.

## PoC Brief

Build the auto-reconciliation tool and show it to some brokers.
